data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "A volcanic winter initiated by the Hekla 3 or Santorini eruption triggered hemispheric circulation changes that differentially impacted rainfall-dependent polities versus Nile-flood agriculture","mechanism": "Volcanic aerosol loading shifted ITCZ position, suppressing winter rainfall in the Eastern Mediterranean while paradoxically enhancing Nile flood intensity through Ethiopian highlands precipitation changes; palace systems with rain-fed dry agriculture collapsed, Egypt benefited","scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states", "Egyptian survival"], "excludes": ["Mesopotamia", "Cyprus", "Mesoamerica"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Speleothem or lake-core records showing no volcanic excursion coinciding with 1200-1150 BC collapse horizon", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Nile flood records from Faiyum or Memphis showing reduced not enhanced inundation during this period", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Hittite capital Hattusa showing no abandonment sequence but rather smooth political transition to Neo-Hittite successor states without material culture rupture", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Typical climate-determinist formulation but with precise geophysical mechanism distinguishing volcanic winter from gradual drought; highly criticizable for requiring specific paleoclimate correlations."}
